title: A Reader's Guide to the Series
thesis: A reader's guide makes the series usable by mapping published articles to the questions different readers bring, rather than forcing a single front-to-back order.

series.title: Attention, Substance, and the AI Moment

claims.claim: The series is organized into six arcs that move from diagnosis through historical framing, the AI opportunity cost, individual practice, systemic design, and synthesis.

evidence.snippet: The series index lists six arcs: The Diagnosis, Historical and Human Frames, The AI Opportunity Cost, Building Substance, Designing for Substance, and Synthesis and Action.

counterevidence.summary: The arc labels are editorial framing, not fixed taxonomies; some articles cross arc boundaries.

claims.claim: The four seed articles cover the core arguments of diagnosis, generational stakes, individual practice, and systemic design.

evidence.snippet: The series index identifies The Attention Extraction, The Generational Bet, The Substance Builder, and Designing for Substance as the seed articles that anchor the series.
The Attention Extraction provides the diagnosis of how India's digital-access success has inverted into attention extraction.
The Generational Bet frames the AI moment as a habit problem and a compounding national wager.
The Substance Builder gives practical, low-fractice guidance for turning dead time into small acts of creation.
Designing for Substance explores platform incentives, alternative metrics, regulation, and product alternatives.

counterevidence.summary: The seed articles are not the only articles that address these themes; later deep-dives add evidence and nuance.

claims.claim: Most readers will find the series more useful if they enter by question or role rather than reading front to back, because the articles are designed to be self-contained.

evidence.snippet: The reader's guide structures entry points by question and by role, and notes that articles can stand alone.

counterevidence.summary: Some readers may prefer a linear read, and cross-references assume at least passing familiarity with earlier concepts.

claims.claim: The series explicitly distinguishes India-specific survey data from global benchmarks and industry estimates.

evidence.snippet: The series index states that every claim is framed with caution, correlational language is used where appropriate, and global figures are labeled as benchmarks applied to India.
By the Numbers labels figures from NSSO, ASER, NCAER, and Gallup with scope and caveat notes, and distinguishes India-specific surveys from global benchmarks.

counterevidence.summary: Not every data callout carries a full three-part caveat label; some sentences rely on context for the confidence signal.

claims.claim: This guide links only to articles that are already published and will be updated as new articles go live.

evidence.snippet: The reader's guide states that it links only to published articles and mentions future articles in plain text without links.

counterevidence.summary: The guide may become outdated between updates if new articles publish before the guide is refreshed.

sources.title: A Reader's Guide to the Series
Attention, Substance, and the AI Moment: A Series Index
The Attention Extraction: How India's Digital Dividend Is Becoming a Cognitive Deficit
By the Numbers: What Indians Actually Do Online
The Generational Bet: Will India Build the AI Age or Scroll Through It?
The Substance Builder: How to Turn Dead Time into Small Acts of Creation
Designing for Substance: Platform Incentives and the Attention Economy

agentInstructions: Use claim IDs as the retrieval unit and route readers to the specific article that answers their question.
Do not link to articles that are not yet published; mention them in plain text if needed.
Preserve the navigational, non-preachy tone; the guide is a map, not a sermon.
When citing evidence, use the source IDs and URLs from the artifact; do not generalize beyond the published articles.
Flag that the guide is a living document and may need updating as the series grows.

reviews.notes: Human author approved publication; links limited to published articles only.

accepted.rationale: Accepted as a quality backlog item per docs/diagnostics-triage-2026-07-15.md. The claim rests on sources that are sufficient for the current published version; additional independent sources will be added in a future research wave.
